draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: For a freelancer CRM, a soft, inviting palette reduces interface fatigue during long client management sessions
facade.label: Warm cream background
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward comfort and approachability; likely prefers human-centered, gentle design language; may value emotional resonance over stark clarity
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers cooler, more neutral or modern palettes; may see warmth as unprofessional or cluttered; likely leans toward minimalist, clinical interface tone

evidence.hypothesis: For a freelancer CRM, a soft, inviting palette reduces interface fatigue during long client management sessions
evidence.implication: User gravitates toward comfort and approachability; likely prefers human-centered, gentle design language; may value emotional resonance over stark clarity

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer auto-saving form inputs without explicit submit buttons, reducing friction in data entry workflows
facade.axisTargeted: Gesture vs click — does the CRM feel frictionless (auto-save, real-time) or deliberate (explicit actions, confirmation steps)?
facade.label: Instant form save
facade.acceptImplies: User values seamless, background data persistence and trusts the system to capture changes without confirmation
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers explicit save actions, clear feedback on what changed, and control over when data commits to the database

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer conversational language over corporate jargon in their CRM interface, making the tool feel like a helpful peer rather than enterprise software.
facade.label: Casual and direct
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into conversational microcopy, relaxed color palettes, and approachable iconography throughout the product
facade.rejectImplies: Explore more polished, formal language and professional visual treatments; consider that freelancers may want to feel taken seriously by their CRM

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer visual card layouts over dense contact tables for quick scanning and action
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values skimmable, visual organization of client info; likely wants card-based UI throughout CRM for people/projects
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes data density and comparison; may prefer tabular views, spreadsheet-like layouts, or detailed text-heavy client profiles

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers value compact, distraction-free workspaces that maximize canvas area for client data
facade.label: Minimal sidebar
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers left-aligned navigation that doesn't compete with content; likely favors sparse, utilitarian design
facade.rejectImplies: User wants visible, prominent navigation or prefers centered layouts; may value explicit structure over minimalism

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need persistent project context while scrolling clients — a fixed sidebar showing active project keeps mental model intact
facade.label: Sidebar with card stack
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick project switching and always-visible status. Design should include persistent left panel with collapsible project list; main area uses card-based client/invoice layout.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers immersive single-task flow. Explore full-screen tab navigation or modal-based project switching instead.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style for solo freelancers
Whether user prefers data shown as numbers/tables vs. visual summaries and charts—reveals how they process client info, invoices, and project status
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. batch processing and summaries—reveals if they prefer snappy interactions or prefer to review changes in bulk

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style for solo freelancers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data shown as numbers/tables vs. visual summaries and charts—reveals how they process client info, invoices, and project status
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. batch processing and summaries—reveals if they prefer snappy interactions or prefer to review changes in bulk
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: For a freelancer CRM, a persistent left navigation with muted colors and clear sections creates a stable command center; reduces cognitive load during rapid client switching
facade.axisTargeted: Warm vs Cool + Calm vs Energetic
facade.label: Calm sidebar
facade.acceptImplies: User values predictable structure and visual quietness; prefers stability over novelty in workspace design; likely works long hours and needs restful interface patterns
facade.rejectImplies: User may want more dynamic, glanceable information architecture; prefers urgency signals and animated feedback; may find static sidebars monotonous or constraining
